Couples Retreat (2009)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $109,205,660Details
International Box Office $63,244,763
Worldwide Box Office $172,450,423
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $39,677,422
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $7,731,286
Total $47,408,708
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Four Midwestern couples embark on a journey to a tropical island resort. While one of the couples is there to work on their marriage, the other three set out to jet ski, spa and enjoy some fun in the sun. They soon discover that participation in the resort’s couple’s therapy is not optional. Suddenly, their group-rate vacation comes at a price. What follows is a hilarious look at real world problems faced by all couples.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$34,286,740 (31.4% of total gross)
Legs:3.19 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:63.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$60,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,000 opening theaters/3,074 max. theaters, 7.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $173,750,204

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 9th, 2009 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: October 8th, 2009 (Wide) (Australia)
Video Release: February 9th, 2010 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sexual content and language (on appeal, originally rated R)
(Rating bulletin 2087 (Cert #45636), 9/16/2009)
Running Time: 113 minutes
Plot point: Group Therapy, Relationship Advice, Romance
Social setting: Vacation
Subgenre: Romantic Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Romantic Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Relativity Media, Stuber Pictures, Wild West Picture Show, Universal Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

  • Summary

Synopsis

Four Midwestern couples embark on a journey to a tropical island resort. While one of the couples is there to work on their marriage, the other three set out to jet ski, spa and enjoy some fun in the sun. They soon discover that participation in the resort’s couple’s therapy is not optional. Suddenly, their group-rate vacation comes at a price. What follows is a hilarious look at real world problems faced by all couples.
